stimulus: Archaeologists have found a roughly 9,000-year-old stone tool from a member of an early human community on a small island off the coast of Greece, suggesting that these communities reached more distant locations than earlier evidence indicated. Before the discovery, these communities were thought to have stayed close to the mainland, traveling only short distances along sheltered coastlines, but the discovery of the tool on the island suggests that they may have crossed open sea over significant distances as well.
stem: Which choice best states the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It provides context that clarifies the significance of the information that follows in the rest of the sentence.
It dismisses as untrue the research presented in the previous sentence.
It defines a term used in the description that follows in the rest of the sentence.
It emphasizes the main goal of the research introduced in the previous sentence.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The underlined portion describes what was previously believed about these early communities, helping the reader understand why the discovery of the tool on a distant island is significant: it suggests they traveled farther than once thought.

Choice B is incorrect. The underlined portion doesn't dismiss research; it explains what was previously believed. Choice C is incorrect. No term is defined. Choice D is incorrect. The text never describes the goal of the research.
